name: Joe Darensbourg
abstract: Joe Darensbourg (July 9, 1906 – May 24, 1985) was an American, New Orleans–based jazz clarinetist and saxophonist, notable for his work with Buddy Petit, Jelly Roll Morton, Charlie Creath, Fate Marable, Andy Kirk, Kid Ory, Wingy Manone, Joe Liggins and Louis Armstrong.

interviews.title: Joe Darensbourg and Minor "Ram" Hall Interview, 1957-04-18

concerts.label: Kid Ory and His Creole Jazz Band

works.t: Bill Bailey, Won't You Please Come Home?
I'm Busy and You Can't Come In

quotes.text: uh, Carnegie Hall or something. He had some high-powered speech, he would tell there.
quotes.caption: Vague recollection of a speech at Carnegie Hall
quotes.doc_title: Joe Darensbourg and Minor "Ram" Hall Interview, 1957-04-18

about_quotes.speaker: Andrew Blakeney
about_quotes.evidence: The personnel with the Legends was 'Montudie' Garland on bass, Louis Nelson on trombone, Joe Darensbourg on clarinet, myself on trumpet...

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: And we had Billy Kyle Arvell Shaw, Joe Darensbourg, Danny Barcelona, we had Jules Brown singing -- beautiful group.

about_quotes.speaker: Evan Christopher
about_quotes.evidence: one of them was a Louisiana clarinetist... His name was Joe Darensbourg. And he ended up on the west coast. And Joe had a wonderful kind of Creole sound on his clarinet and it was I remember one of the first times I remember being very conscious that this is different than like Benny Goodman...
about_quotes.doc_title: Evan Christopher interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 1, 2009

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Vacher
about_quotes.evidence: I brought the book I wrote about Joe Darensbourg... I interviewed him... Following on from this moment with Darensbourg, I spent a lot of time in Los Angeles with Joe when we were starting to get all the material for this together
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Vacher interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Garland
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...let's say if you're playing with Barney Bigard and you were playing for Joe Darensbourg? [INTERVIEWEE] Uh-huh.
